name: Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda
name_original: Vuelta al Azud de Riolobos desde El Campo de Peñaranda
region_intro: Hiking around El Campo De Peñaranda offers routes through a predominantly flat, agricultural landscape in the province of Salamanca. The region is characterized by open fields, small villages, and the presence of water features like the Azud de Riolobos. Elevation changes are minimal, making for generally accessible trails. The area provides opportunities for exploring the rural Spanish countryside. Best hiking trails around El Campo De Peñaranda The most popular hiking route is Ride to Azud de Riolobos, an 8.2 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to a local reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop, a moderate 6.6 miles (10.6 km) path. This loop connects two towns through the rural landscape. Local hikers also love the Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through open countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around El Campo De Peñaranda is defined by agricultural fields, quiet rural roads, and small water features. The network offers options primarily for moderate ability levels. The routes in El Campo De Peñaranda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore El Campo De Peñaranda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: There are currently 6 hiking routes documented in El Campo De Peñaranda on komoot. These trails primarily traverse the region's flat, agricultural landscape.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hiking routes in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: All the hiking routes in El Campo De Peñaranda are rated as moderate. However, given the predominantly flat terrain and minimal elevation changes, they are generally accessible and suitable for hikers with a basic level of fitness, including beginners looking for slightly longer walks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: The best time to hike in El Campo De Peñaranda is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and the countryside is vibrant. Winter can also be pleasant for hiking due to the generally flat terrain, while summer months (July-August) can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282796959/penaranda-de-bracamonte-and-cantaracillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaranda de Bracamonte and Cantaracillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; connects two towns through the rural landscape, offering a great way to experience the region's charm. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20141628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The hiking trails in El Campo De Peñaranda are characterized by open agricultural fields, quiet rural roads, and small villages. You'll experience the tranquil Spanish countryside, with minimal forest cover and gentle, rolling landscapes. Water features like the Azud de Riolobos are also part of the scenery.

Q: Are the hiking trails in El Campo De Peñaranda suitable for families?
A: Given the predominantly flat terrain and moderate difficulty of the routes, many trails in El Campo De Peñaranda can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to walking longer distances. The lack of significant climbs makes them less strenuous than mountain hikes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or places to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Along and near the hiking routes, you can explore several historical and natural points of interest. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel of Peñaranda de Bracamonte&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6063447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Assumption&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riolobos Weir&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant water feature that several routes lead to or pass by, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993179392/ride-to-azud-de-riolobos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to Azud de Riolobos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in this region?
A: No, there are generally no specific hiking permits required for the trails in El Campo De Peñaranda. The routes typically follow public paths and rural roads through agricultural land.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: The komoot community rates the trails in El Campo De Peñaranda highly, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often appreciate the peaceful, open countryside and the opportunity to experience authentic rural Spain away from crowded tourist areas. The accessibility of the routes is also frequently mentioned.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of water features?
A: Yes, the region features water bodies like the Azud de Riolobos. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282873292/return-to-azud-de-riolobos-from-el-campo-de-penaranda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Azud de Riolobos from El Campo de Peñaranda&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads to this weir, offering views of the water and surrounding landscape.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in El Campo De Peñaranda?
A: For hiking in El Campo De Peñaranda, comfortable walking shoes or light hiking boots are recommended, as the terrain is generally flat but can involve uneven paths. Layered clothing is advisable to adapt to changing temperatures, especially during spring and autumn. Don't forget sun protection, as many routes are exposed to the sun.

Q: Is there public transport access to the trailheads?
A: While specific public transport details for each trailhead are not provided, the routes often start or pass through small towns and villages like Peñaranda de Bracamonte, which may have local bus connections. It's advisable to check local transport schedules if you plan to rely on public transport.
tips: Parish church dedicated to the Assumption of Our Lady, built in the 16th century.

The interior of the Castilian plateau has these remote places but with great landscape and environmental value and interest. The Azud de Riolobos is an ideal place for those who enjoy bird watching or simply to appreciate this beautiful landscape surrounded by great tranquility.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
